verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 (Q1ZYL8) is a member of the IZUMO family but is atypical. Unlike IZUMO1 (sperm-surface single-pass TM protein required for fertilization), IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain and is annotated as nuclear-localized by the NCBI/Alliance of Genome Resources summary. UniProt Q1ZYL8 is a small protein (~150 aa) with no predicted TM or GPI anchor. Checking contextual buckets: cell_state_induced — no evidence of stress/activation-induced surface display; tissue_restricted_surface — no surface evidence in any lineage; lysosomal_exocytosis — no supporting data; dual_localization — no PM pool documented; stable_surface_attachment — no TM partner reported. No surface biotinylation, flow cytometry on intact cells, or therapeutic targeting programs were identified. The nuclear localization annotation and absence of any membrane-anchoring domain support a nuclear/cytoplasmic localization with no extracellular face accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; nuclear annotation is from automated pipeline and no experimental localization data rules out any cytoplasmic/membrane pool unambiguously.
